surfaces.rationale: Sole landing board; no iterations. Maps cleanly to the unauthenticated / branch. CRT scanlines drawn as 140 lines; contract wants 3 CRT layers + 5-layer Glitch404 — additive at build time, not a design fork.
Single canonical two-screen + states-annotation board. or-divider and Google OAuth button duplicated identically across both cards — extract as reusables.

Clean 5-state collection (empty/submitting/3 errors). CAMP-XXXX-XXXX slug placeholder aligns with the human-readable invite-code redesign intent. Note: this is the GATE (consume a code); the named/multi-use/pre-approve MINTING redesign lives on S14, not here.

S04 is the OLD all-pages-on-one-tall-canvas annotation board; the per-step OB Step 01–11 frames are the known redesign (per-step pages). OB series wins. S04 retains unique value as the only place the full footer-state matrix (page-0 / middle / last / submitting / error-banner / inline-error / save-failed) is documented — port those states onto the per-step pages at build. S05 Field kinds is the field-renderer source of truth feeding both onboarding and the questionnaire runner. UNRESOLVED (implementation, not a design choice): OB shows 'Step N of 11' while contract+S04 say 12 pages. OB Step 05 IS the intro page, so 11 likely just omits the intro from the visible counter.

Designer reference sheet for all 10 question kinds; not routable. Two genuine design-vs-code mismatches to resolve at build: toggle drawn as independent binary switches (code = segmented radiogroup) and scale drawn as segmented buttons (code = Radix slider). These are spec reconciliations, not competing iterations.
Canonical two-state (pending/rejected) collection. Sign out is the only action — matches the dead-end intent. No iterations.

S08 and S07 share the same stacked rank-section layout, the same tile labels (incl. the Crew Roster/Tasks/Forms/Announcements set that diverges from contract), and both drop the old quadrant + TALK + tab-bar. S08 is strictly the more developed successor: it adds rank-coloured group chips, per-group tool-count, a Customize/drag-to-reorder mode with DropSlot, a Pinned group + DropZone, and a New Group affordance — a superset of S07. S07 is the simpler precursor and is superseded. ORPHAN: neither board has a TALK/push-to-talk centre, so voice loses its home entry point (see orphanRisks). Both home boards omit the contract's visible-but-locked treatment for higher ranks (S07 uses opacity+VIEW ONLY; S08 shows all groups full-opacity) — reconcile the rank-lock affordance at build.
Single canonical populated snapshot with all three nav actions (edit / questionnaire review / sign out). RankPill fill token (#ff008c2e) diverges from contract's $secondary — token fix, not a fork.

Single canonical two-card screen (profile + danger zone). S11 Avatar upload is the embedded avatar sub-component's state showcase (empty/uploading/populated/error) consumed here and in onboarding step 1 — supporting, not a route.

Component state showcase, not a page. AvatarUpload also appears inline on OB Step 01 — formalise as one shared component. ERROR block omits the re-upload button that the coded component keeps; add it back.
Single canonical inbox showing all 3 broadcast presentations (acknowledge/popup/feed) in unread+read, plus inline empty state. Uses DetailHeader. No iterations.

Unique canonical hub, 3 NavCards (Invite / My forms / Family tree). Stale 'single-use' invite copy must be updated to reflect the multi-use named-slug redesign. NavCard pattern repeats here, S14, S19 — extract.

Single canonical board carrying the invite-code redesign: human-readable slug (neon-toaster-mongoose), note field, captain-only pre-approve checkbox + multi-use stepper, member notice, live availability hint (available/invalid/taken/submitting), and a success card. This is where the named/multi-use/pre-approve intent actually lands. GAP to reconcile at build: contract's email field is entirely absent; member (non-captain) variant with CaptainOptions hidden is not drawn.

Single canonical board showing list (FormCard + EmptyState) and detail (SavedBanner + ChangeLog populated/empty). Notable omission: the questionnaire wizard replay shell itself is not mocked — it reuses the S05/runner field machine, so not a separate design.

Single canonical 720px board (wider shell is intentional per contract). Recursive collapsible tree with via-slug lines, captain/you pills, descendant counts, match-highlight + two empty states. This IS the family-tree rework intent (no competing iteration on the canvas). Captain/match-highlight use $accent vs contract's amber — token reconciliation.

surfaces.canonicalBoard: S17 Roster — Iteration B (responsive: terminal-console desktop + mobile)
surfaces.rationale: [RESOLVED by LOCKED decision 2 — Iteration B is canonical & responsive: desktop = terminal-console board, mobile = mobile board; S17 Captain mgmt (Iteration A) is DROPPED, not a companion. Triage note retained below for provenance.] Three iterations of one surface. Given Camp 404's mobile-first 430px mandate, the mobile Iteration B is the natural canonical: it carries the full feature superset (stats strip, multi-chip filters, inline member profile, two-sided Assign-captain flow, reject confirm) at the target width AND instantiates the shared CaptainLock component for the locked state. S17 Captain mgmt (1040px) and the terminal-console (1040px) are desktop-wide tables that violate the mobile-first constraint and are kept as supporting references — BUT the terminal-console's distinct JetBrains-Mono art direction is a real aesthetic choice escalated as a fork. The 1040px table is not dead: a captain on desktop benefits from it, so treat Captain mgmt as the desktop-wide companion to the mobile canonical. ALL THREE add the Assign-captain double-opt-in flow and drop the contract's Questionnaires/Driver/In-SA boolean columns — that captain-promotion feature needs a new captain_promotion_request entity at build.
Sole board for the surface: composer + drafts list + published list, presentation selector open to document all 3 delivery modes. Canonical, not an iteration.

Unique canonical hub. Board proposes a locked/dimmed CaptainLock view for non-captains — diverges from contract's hard redirect-to-/. This soft-lock matches the S17 roster locked treatment, so it is a coherent product decision worth adopting. Implies a planned second tool (Roster & approvals) in the TOOLS array.

Single board collapsing the bridge page + consent prompt + 403 pending gate into one frame. Canonical; additional denial branches (onboarding-incomplete, no-camp-access) and loading/expired states need designing at build.

surfaces.route: n/a (RecorderPanel embedded in long_text fields + bug dialog; possible /…/dictate modal)
surfaces.rationale: State showcase for all recorder states (idle/requesting/recording/processing/error) plus a NEW transcript-review step (Re-record / Use this text) not in the contract's silent-append behaviour. This is the ONLY remaining home for voice after the home TALK centre was removed (see orphanRisks). The DictatePill affordance on OB Step 03/04 is the entry trigger that launches this panel — wire them together.

Showcase of 5 floating surfaces: AckTakeover, QuestionnaireBlock, ShakeReporter, ErrorBoundary, Toast. ShakeReporter is drawn at lower fidelity than the coded ReportBugDialog (no kind toggle, no voice, no AI checkbox, no success view) — enrich at build. QuestionnaireBlock overlaps S25 gate conceptually; treat S25 as the routed gate and this as the app-blocking overlay variant.
State-machine doc for the EnablePush component: one visible state (default) + four null-render states. Carried unchanged into both home boards. Supporting component, not a route.
Pure design-system reference; the single source for type/colour tokens and three patterns that appear nowhere else (segmented-control, inline-alert, ghost/destructive button variants). Not a screen.

Entry of the S25 gate → S26 runner → S27 complete trio. A NEW pre-wizard interstitial not in the contract (which routes straight into the wizard). Decide at build whether to keep it as a distinct intro step or fold into the wizard's page 0; it is not a competing iteration of any other board.

The blocking-gate runner with BlockingTopBar, BlockingNotice, progress, and a docs section cataloguing question-kind + interaction states. Distinct from the onboarding wizard (OB Step series) by its required-action blocking chrome. Shares the field machine with S05. Scale-as-segments vs Radix-slider mismatch carries over from S05.

Post-submission landing closing the S25→S26→S27 trio. Introduces a multi-questionnaire sequential-unlock queue (Safety/Dietary/Agreements) beyond the contract's single burner_profile — a scope expansion to confirm, not a competing iteration. success-hero is a strong shared-component candidate.

forks.question: [RESOLVED — LOCKED decision 2: Iteration B responsive (terminal-console desktop + mobile); Iteration A (S17 Captain mgmt) dropped.] Which roster iteration is canonical — the mobile-first card list, the desktop-wide standard table, or the desktop-wide terminal-console aesthetic? This is the explicit '3 roster iterations' decision and it carries a genuine art-direction split.

options.summary: 430px mobile-first card list: stats strip, multi-chip filters, inline expandable member profile, two-sided Assign-captain flow, reject confirm, CaptainLock for the locked state.
options.pros: Matches the camp's mobile-first 430px mandate exactly; reuses the shared CaptainLock component; carries the full feature superset; one-handed phone-in-the-desert ergonomics from the north-star.
options.cons: Inline-expand profile is less scannable than a wide table for a captain triaging 42 people; loses the at-a-glance multi-column comparison.

options.summary: 1040px standard shadcn-style wide table (Member/Handle/Country/Role/Status/View), inline member profile panel, Assign-captain + reject dialogs, CaptainLock locked state.
options.pros: Best information density for triaging a whole roster at once; closest to the contract's wide-table intent (max-w-5xl); uses CaptainLock; calm neutral styling consistent with the rest of the app.
options.cons: 1040px breaks the mobile-first constraint as the PRIMARY surface; needs a separate mobile layout anyway, so cannot be the sole canonical.

options.summary: 1040px wide table with a full JetBrains-Mono terminal aesthetic: TermBar address widget, '>' prompts, blinking carets, per-row status bars; rolls its own read-only panel instead of CaptainLock.
options.pros: Strong, memorable art direction that leans into the 'Camp 404' / glitch brand; richest stats + filter affordances; reads as a power-user captain console.
options.cons: Heaviest divergence from the calm-command-centre north-star and the rest of the app's Inter-based styling; 1040px breaks mobile-first; does NOT use the shared CaptainLock (duplicate read-only pattern); highest build cost.

forks.recommendation: Adopt S17 Roster — Iteration B (mobile) as the canonical surface (mobile-first mandate + shared CaptainLock), and keep S17 Captain mgmt as the desktop-wide companion layout for the same route. Drop the terminal-console aesthetic for the production roster UNLESS the camp wants the terminal art direction as the app-wide identity — that is the human call. All three agree on the Assign-captain double-opt-in flow and the dropped boolean columns, so the feature set is settled regardless of which skin wins.

forks.question: [RESOLVED — LOCKED decision 3: preview-but-locked CaptainLock — navigable, structure renders, NO data, inert controls; replaces the hard redirect.] When a non-captain reaches a captain-only surface, do they get a hard redirect to / (contract) or a visible-but-locked CaptainLock view (boards)?

options.summary: Render the captain surface dimmed with a CaptainLock card ('captains only / your rank doesn't include it') instead of bouncing the user. Same pattern used on the S17 roster boards.
options.pros: Matches the north-star 'rank is clearance, not a wall — layers stay visible but locked'; consistent across roster + tools + home rank sections; lets a member see that captain tooling exists.
options.cons: Diverges from the contract's documented hard redirect on /captains/* ; shows the existence of captain features to members (minor info disclosure).

options.summary: feature-set contract behaviour: rank !== captain → redirect('/'); no locked view is ever rendered on captain routes.
options.pros: Simplest to implement; zero info disclosure; already the coded behaviour today.
options.cons: Contradicts the canvas's consistent soft-lock language AND the overview §4 captain-only-locked global state, which itself prescribes 'visible but locked'.

forks.recommendation: Adopt the boards' visible-but-locked CaptainLock pattern. The §4 global-states matrix in the overview prescribes 'render the surface visible but locked' for captain-only-locked, so the boards are more aligned with the stated invariant than the current redirect code. Update the captain routes to soft-lock rather than redirect.

componentInventory: IconChip / IconBadge (bordered tinted icon container; tools + gate screens)
AvatarUpload (avatar circle + camera placeholder + upload/remove; S10/S11/OB Step 01)
ProgressBar (Step N of M label + track/fill; all OB steps + S04 + S26)
combobox-field (searchable popover with flag rows; S04/S05/OB Step 02)
DictatePill (mic + 'Dictate instead'; OB Step 03/04, launches S21)
notification-row (icon-circle + text + New pill + attribution; S12)
roster-row (avatar + name + handle + country + role; S17 all three)
stat-tile (label + big mono number; S17 mobile/terminal)
two-step progress tracker (Assign-captain double opt-in; S17 all three)
tree-row (avatar + pills + via-code + count + toggle; S16)
role-group-card (chip + label + tool-count + tile grid; S08)
AckTakeover / QuestionnaireBlock / ShakeReporter / ErrorBoundary / Toast overlays (S22)
orphanRisks: VOICE LOST ITS HOME: the old home push-to-talk TALK centre is removed from BOTH home iterations (S07 and S08). The contract calls voice 'the centre of the home control panel' and a first-class input. After redesign, voice survives only as (a) the RecorderPanel/S21 reached via the DictatePill on long_text fields (OB Step 03/04, S05) and (b) the bug-report dialog. There is NO standalone voice entry on home. DECISION NEEDED at build: is field-level dictation a sufficient home for voice, or does home need a re-homed mic affordance? The §7 contract is functionally satisfied ('voice input must remain reachable'), but the design intent of voice-as-hero is lost.

S21 Voice dictation has no firm route — documented as an embedded panel but drawn as a full page with its own back-nav and a new transcript-review step; needs a host (inline panel vs modal route) decided.

S25 Questionnaire gate and S22's QuestionnaireBlock overlay both gate on questionnaire completion with no clear single owner — one is a routed interstitial, the other an app-wide overlay; risk of two parallel implementations of the same gate.

S04 Onboarding wizard's footer-state matrix (page-0 / middle / last / submitting / error-banner / inline-error / save-failed) has no home once S04 is superseded — these states are NOT collectively redrawn on the per-step OB boards and could be dropped silently.

S17 Captain mgmt (1040px) and S17 terminal-console (1040px) become orphaned screens if the mobile iteration is chosen as sole canonical and no desktop-wide layout is retained for the captain roster route.
Tools hub (S13) Invite card still reads 'single-use' — a stale copy route-in that contradicts the multi-use named-slug redesign on the S14 destination; entry-point and destination disagree.

Several home tiles (Camp Tasks, Crew Tasks, Finances, Crew Forms, Crew Announcements) point to surfaces with NO board on the canvas — tiles with no destination screen (tasks, finances, team-scoped forms/announcements are undrawn).
S20 MCP consent's onboarding-incomplete and no-camp-access 403 branches are referenced but undrawn — gated states with no page.
notes: Census is 50 boards: 10 reusable components, plus primitive/component showcases (S05, S24, S11, S23) and the rest page-screens or state collections. Net result: 26 logical surfaces, exactly two genuine human forks (roster art-direction; non-captain gating), and a short list of build-time reconciliations that are NOT forks (token mismatches, scale/toggle render kind, step-count 11-vs-12, missing email field on invite, enriching ShakeReporter). Redesign intents are all accounted for: (a) quadrant+TALK removed → S08 canonical home, voice flagged as orphan; (b) per-step onboarding → OB Step 01–11 supersedes S04; (c) named/multi-use/pre-approve invites → S14 (gate S03 only consumes a code); (d) roster → 3 iterations escalated as the one real fork; (e) family tree → S16 is the sole rework, no competing take. Zero truly redundant screens remain after S07→S08 and S04→OB collapses; the two 1040px rosters are reframed as a desktop companion rather than discarded so the route keeps both a mobile and a desktop layout. Confidence is high on everything except the questionnaire trio (S25/S26/S27 add scope beyond the single-burner-profile contract and need a product confirmation) and the roster fork (genuine human call). Key build reconciliations: design-token drift ($accent vs amber on tree/announcements; #ff008c2e RankPill vs $secondary; raw hex tints vs semantic tokens on GridTile), and the home rank-lock affordance (S07 opacity vs S08 full-opacity vs contract visible-but-locked) — adopt the §4 visible-but-locked grammar."
